cards.notes: είμαι. Irregular medial verb. Singular: είμαι/είσαι/είναι. Plural: είμαστε/είστε/είναι.
πίνω. Paradigm: πίνω, πίνεις, πίνει, πίνουμε, πίνετε, πίνουν.
τρώω. Paradigm: τρώω, τρως, τρώει, τρώμε, τρώτε, τρώνε.
πηγαίνω. Short form πάω is also common.
έχω. Paradigm: έχω, έχεις, έχει, έχουμε, έχετε, έχουν.
μιλάω / μιλώ. Class-2 verb. Μιλάς ελληνικά; = Do you speak Greek?
περιμένω. Example: Σε περιμένω εδώ (I'm waiting for you here).
θέλω. Paradigm: θέλω, θέλεις, θέλει, θέλουμε, θέλετε, θέλουν.
κάνω = do / make / cost. Πόσο κάνει; = How much?
επιμένω. Built from μένω + επι- prefix.

cards.concept: Verb — I look at / I watch

cards.notes: αγαπώ. Class-2 verb. 'Se' elides to Σ' before α.
χρειάζομαι. MID-VOICE verb. Χρειάζομαι βοήθεια = I need help.
διαβάζω. Past simple: διάβασα. Past continuous: διάβαζα.
μένω. Also 'live (in a place)'. Μένω στην Αθήνα = I live in Athens.
Aorist 1sg of τρώω. Present: τρώω; past continuous: έτρωγα.
Impersonal: literally 'to-me it-pleases'. For plural subject: Μου αρέσουν.
Aorist 1sg of ξεκινάω. Pattern: drop -άω, add -ησα.

cards.concept: Verb — I don't want to know
cards.notes: θέλω + να + verb replaces the missing infinitive. Literally: 'I-don't-want that I-know'.
Imperfect (past continuous) 1sg of διαβάζω. Example: Διάβαζα ένα βιβλίο όταν μου τηλεφώνησες.
μπορώ = can. βοηθάω = help. Pattern: μπορώ + να + verb.
θα + present stem = continuous future. θα + perfective stem = simple future (Θα δω).
Past simple of έχω. Paradigm: είχα, είχες, είχε, είχαμε, είχατε, είχαν.
καταλαβαίνω = understand. Universally useful tourist phrase.
Past of είμαι. Paradigm: ήμουν, ήσουν, ήταν, ήμασταν, ήσασταν, ήταν.
Polite request. θα + ήθελα (past of θέλω, used as conditional). Θα ήθελα έναν καφέ = I'd like a coffee.
PERFECT TENSE: έχω + perfective infinitive form (-ει). Means completed action with present relevance.
πήγαμε = 1st plural aorist (past simple) of πηγαίνω. Stress shifts back in aorist.
Idiom. Lit. 'Look at your work.' Imperative of κοιτάω.
Common future expression. θα + δούμε (perfective 1pl of βλέπω).
NEUTER despite referring to a male. Diminutive endings -ι / -άκι usually → neuter.
Feminine. η ημέρα. Καλημέρα = good day.
NEUTER despite referring to a female. Diminutive ending.
Neuter article: το. Neuter nouns in -ι are very common.
Neuter. Plural: τα παιδιά. Means child OR kid OR boyfriend/girlfriend depending on context.
Neuter. Greek 'school' = origin of English word.
Singular το παιδί → plural τα παιδιά. Article το → τα.

en: What case is 'ton filo' in 'Vlepo ton filo mou'?
cards.notes: βλέπω takes a direct object → accusative. ο φίλος → τον φίλο.
Singular ο άνδρας → plural οι άνδρες. Article ο → οι.
Genitive singular of το σπίτι. Used for possession: το κλειδί του σπιτιού = the house key.
Accusative (direct object). Nom ο φίλος → acc τον φίλο.
σε + το → στο. σε requires accusative case.
Singular η γυναίκα → plural οι γυναίκες. Article η → οι.
Neuter -ί declension. Same pattern as το σπίτι.
Masculine -ές noun. Note inserted -δ- in plural.
Neuter declension. Nom = acc in neuter.
-ας ending masculine. Genitive plural stress shifts to ending.
Feminine -α. Genitive plural stress shifts: γυναικών.
Possessive μου AFTER the noun. Greek possessives are weak pronouns attached to the noun phrase.
Possessive σου (informal, singular) AFTER the noun.
VOCATIVE case for direct address. ο φίλος → φίλε! (drop the -ς, change vowel).
Formal goodbye. Γεια σου is also used informally.
Lit. 'to our health.' Shorter: Γεια μας!
Used until early afternoon. Καλη- (good) + μέρα (day).
Said when parting at night / before sleep.
Multi-purpose: please, you're welcome, come in, can I help you?
All-purpose: excuse me, sorry, pardon. Also Συγγνώμη; with a question intonation = pardon?
Ευχαριστώ πολύ = thank you very much.
Έναν is accusative masc. of one (έναν / μία / ένα).

cards.concept: Small talk — Where are you from?
cards.notes: από + πού + είσαι = from + where + you-are.
από + accusative (την). Other countries: από την Αγγλία (England), από τον Καναδά (Canada).
Lit. 'They call me…'. Με λένε Γιάννη = My name is Yiannis.
Lit. 'How do they call you?' Formal: Πώς σας λένε;

cards.concept: Small talk — Nice to meet you
cards.notes: Lit. 'I rejoice greatly.' Said upon being introduced.
Often add Ένα μπουκάλι νερό = a bottle of water.
η σακούλα = bag (the cashier always asks).
Aorist of χορταίνω = to be sated. Said at the end of a meal.
Lit. 'many years!' Also for name-days & holidays.
Lit. 'it makes much heat.' η ζέστη = heat.

cards.concept: Small talk — How was your day?
cards.notes: η μέρα σου = your day. ήταν = was.
Aorist of λείπω = to be missing. Lit. 'you-were-missing to-me.'
ο καιρός = weather / time. ωραίος = nice (m.).

cards.concept: Travel — Where is the bus stop?
cards.notes: η στάση = stop. Specifically: η στάση του λεωφορείου = bus stop.
Polite/plural form of έχω. Έχετε αυτό σε άλλο χρώμα; = Do you have this in another color?
Also: Πόσο κάνει; (very common in markets).
σήμερα = today. Compare εχθές (yesterday) and αύριο (tomorrow).
Lit. 'take me later'. παίρνω τηλέφωνο = to call.
Lit. 'good appetite.' Said before everyone starts eating.
μέτριος = with a moderate amount of sugar. Other options: σκέτος (no sugar), γλυκύς (sweet).

cards.concept: Café — Frappé with milk and sugar
en: A frappé with milk and sugar.
gr: Έναν φραπέ με γάλα και ζάχαρη.
phon: Enan frape me gala kai zahari.

en: Do you have fresh fish today?

cards.concept: Plans — Want to grab a coffee?
Grocery — Half a kilo of cheese
en: I want half a kilo of cheese.

cards.notes: πεινάω = to be hungry. 2nd sg πεινάς.

en: Give me a kilo of tomatoes.
cards.notes: Δώσε = imperative of δίνω. το κιλό = the kilo.
Imperative of κλείνω. τα φώτα = lights.
Lit. 'how much does the kilo do.'
Lit. 'we tell them tomorrow.' Idiom for 'see you'.
Said to a taxi/bus driver. Lit. 'here, please.'
να + subjunctive of προσέχω = to be careful.
ο κωδικός = code/password. του wifi = genitive.
Imperative of ανοίγω. το παράθυρο = window.
η ώρα = hour / time. Είναι τρεις = it's three.

cards.concept: Travel — When does the bus leave?
en: What time does the bus leave?

cards.notes: θα + perfective = future. τα πιάτα = the plates.
Imperative of κρατάω. τα ρέστα = change.

en: Where did you put the keys?
I have to do the laundry.
cards.notes: πρέπει = must. βάλω πλυντήριο = lit. 'put a washing-machine'.

cards.concept: Small talk — Long time no see
cards.notes: Lit. 'we have time without seeing each other.' Idiom.

cards.concept: Chores — Did you make the bed?
cards.notes: Aorist 2sg of στρώνω. το κρεβάτι = bed.

cards.concept: Phone — Sorry I missed your call
gr: Συγγνώμη που έχασα την κλήση σου.
phon: Signomi pou ehasa tin klisi sou.
cards.notes: η κλήση = call. έχασα = aorist of χάνω = miss.

en: It needs a bit more salt.
cards.notes: Lit. 'it wants a little more salt.'
πληρώνω = pay. με κάρτα = by card.
Imperative of στρώνω = lay out / set.

en: I'll be there in ten minutes.
gr: Θα είμαι εκεί σε δέκα λεπτά.
phon: Tha eimai ekei se deka lepta.
cards.notes: εκεί = there. σε δέκα λεπτά = in ten minutes.
Said to wrap up an order at the deli counter.
το εισιτήριο = ticket. για + acc. = for/to.
Said when leaving a host's table.
Universal emergency cry. βοηθάω = to help (verb).
Imperative plural of καλώ. αστυνομία = police.
Letter 7. Long 'i'. Same sound as Ι and Υ.
Often paired with a slight upward head-tilt.
Often shortened to ντάξει in speech.
WATCH OUT: looks like English 'nay' (no) but means YES.
Letter 8. Unvoiced 'th' as in 'think' — different from Δ!
Letter 15. Short 'o'. Same sound as Ω.
Letter 20. Same sound as Η and Ι.
Letter 22. Rough 'h' like German 'ch' or Spanish 'j'.
Letter 24, the last. Same sound as Ο.
και is used constantly. Often elides before a vowel: κι αυτός.
αλλά = but. Contrast μα = but (more conversational).
